html:
𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Nineteen — Part Four

The Book of Yehezkel

YEHEZKEL

The Book of Ezekiel — 4QEzeka · 4QEzekb · 11QEzek Primary

Chapters 25 through 32

Oracles Against the Nations — Ammon Moav Edom — Tzor the Ship of Beauty — The Prince of Tzor — Egypt the Broken Reed — The Cedar Brought to Sheol

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

4QEzeka · 4QEzekb · 11QEzek — Dead Sea Scrolls — Cave 4 and Cave 11 Qumran

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Complete Canon

YHWH and The Creator only

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTERS 25 THROUGH 32 — ORACLES AGAINST THE NATIONS

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTER 25 Oracles Against Ammon — Moav — Edom — Pelishtim — Because You Said Aha — They Shall Know That I Am YHWH

1 The word of YHWH came to me:

2 Son of man, set your face toward the Ammonites and prophesy against them.

3 Say to the Ammonites, Hear the word of the Creator YHWH: Thus says the Creator YHWH, Because you said, Aha, over my sanctuary when it was profaned, and over the land of Yisra'EL when it was made desolate, and over the house of Yehudah when they went into exile,

4 Therefore behold, I am handing you over to the people of the East for a possession, and they shall set their encampments among you and make their dwellings in your midst. They shall eat your fruit, and they shall drink your milk.

5 I will make Rabbah a pasture for camels and Ammon a fold for flocks. Then you will know that I am YHWH.

6 For thus says the Creator YHWH: Because you have clapped your hands and stamped your feet and rejoiced with all the malice within you against the land of Yisra'EL,

7 Therefore, behold, I have stretched out my hand against you, and will hand you over as plunder to the nations. And I will cut you off from the peoples and will make you perish out of the countries. I will destroy you. Then you will know that I am YHWH.

8 Thus says the Creator YHWH: Because Moav and Se'ir said, Behold, the house of Yehudah is like all the other nations,

9 Therefore I will lay open the flank of Moav from the cities on its frontier, the glory of the country, Beit-Yeshimot, Ba'al-Me'on, and Kiryatayim.

10 I will give it along with Ammon to the people of the East as a possession, that Ammon may be remembered no more among the nations,

11 And I will execute judgments upon Moav. Then they will know that I am YHWH.

12 Thus says the Creator YHWH: Because Edom acted revengefully against the house of Yehudah and has grievously offended in taking vengeance on them,

13 Therefore thus says the Creator YHWH, I will stretch out my hand against Edom and cut off from it man and beast. And I will make it desolate. From Teman even to Dedan they shall fall by the sword.

14 And I will lay my vengeance upon Edom by the hand of my people Yisra'EL, and they shall do in Edom according to my anger and according to my wrath, and they shall know my vengeance, declares the Creator YHWH.

15 Thus says the Creator YHWH: Because the Pelishtim acted revengefully and took vengeance with malice of soul to destroy in never-ending enmity,

16 Therefore thus says the Creator YHWH, Behold, I will stretch out my hand against the Pelishtim, and I will cut off the Kerethites and destroy the rest of the seacoast.

17 I will execute great vengeance on them with wrathful rebukes. Then they will know that I am YHWH, when I lay my vengeance upon them.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS preserves Chapter 25 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The oracles against the four nations surrounding Yehudah — Ammon, Moav, Edom, Pelishtim — are structured around their specific covenant offenses against YHWH's covenant people at the moment of the covenant people's maximum vulnerability. Rejoicing over covenant judgment — Ammon, Moav — covenant revenge — Edom — and ancient covenant enmity — Pelishtim — are each met with covenant judgment.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 25 in full. Verse 3 — because you said, Aha, over my sanctuary when it was profaned. The word Aha — heakh — is the exclamation of malicious satisfaction. The surrounding nations' covenant sin is not merely that they are hostile to Yisra'EL but that they gloat over the destruction of YHWH's covenant sanctuary. The desecration of the covenant place draws the covenant vengeance of YHWH even when inflicted by YHWH's own chosen covenant instrument.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 25. Verse 14 — I will lay my vengeance upon Edom by the hand of my people Yisra'EL. The covenant pattern: Edom's covenant vengeance against Yehudah is answered by YHWH's covenant vengeance through Yisra'EL against Edom. The covenant community becomes the covenant instrument of YHWH's response to covenant injustice done against them.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 8 — because Moav and Se'ir said, Behold, the house of Yehudah is like all the other nations. The covenant theological offense of Moav is the denial of covenant particularity: the claim that Yehudah's destruction proves that the covenant people have no special covenant status. YHWH's covenant response is to demonstrate covenant particularity precisely by judging Moav for denying it. The nations are judged by how they treated and interpreted the covenant people.

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTER 26 The Oracle Against Tzor — She Said Aha — I Will Bring Nevukhadnetzar Against You — Your Walls Shall Tremble — You Shall Be Built No More

1 In the eleventh year, on the first day of the month, the word of YHWH came to me:

2 Son of man, because Tzor said concerning Yerushalayim, Aha, the gate of the peoples is broken. It has swung open to me. I shall be replenished, now that she is laid waste,

3 Therefore thus says the Creator YHWH: Behold, I am against you, O Tzor, and will bring up many nations against you, as the sea brings up its waves.

4 They shall destroy the walls of Tzor and break down her towers, and I will scrape her soil from her and make her a bare rock.

5 She shall be in the midst of the sea a place for the spreading of nets, for I have spoken, declares the Creator YHWH. And she shall become plunder for the nations,

6 And her daughters on the mainland shall be killed by the sword. Then they will know that I am YHWH.

7 For thus says the Creator YHWH: Behold, I will bring against Tzor from the north Nevukhadnetzar king of Babylon, king of kings, with horses and chariots, and with horsemen and a host of many soldiers.

8 He will kill with the sword your daughters on the mainland. He will set up a siege wall against you and throw up a mound against you and raise a roof of shields against you.

9 He will direct the shock of his battering rams against your walls, and with his axes he will break down your towers.

10 His horses will be so many that their dust will cover you. Your walls will shake at the noise of the horsemen and wagons and chariots, when he enters your gates as men enter a city that has been breached.

11 With the hoofs of his horses he will trample all your streets. He will kill your people with the sword, and your mighty pillars will fall to the ground.

12 They will plunder your riches and loot your merchandise. They will break down your walls and destroy your pleasant houses. Your stones and timber and soil they will cast into the midst of the waters.

13 And I will stop the music of your songs, and the sound of your lyres shall be heard no more.

14 I will make you a bare rock. You shall be a place for the spreading of nets. You shall never be rebuilt, for I am YHWH. I have spoken, declares the Creator YHWH.

15 Thus says the Creator YHWH to Tzor: Will not the coastlands shake at the sound of your fall, when the wounded groan, when slaughter is made in your midst?

16 Then all the princes of the sea will step down from their thrones and remove their robes and strip off their embroidered garments. They will clothe themselves with trembling. They will sit on the ground and tremble every moment and be appalled at you.

17 And they will raise a lamentation over you and say to you, How you have perished, you who were inhabited from the seas, O city renowned, who was mighty on the sea, she and her inhabitants, who imposed their terror on all her inhabitants.

18 Now the coastlands tremble on the day of your fall, and the coastlands that are by the sea are dismayed at your passing.

19 For thus says the Creator YHWH: When I make you a city laid waste, like the cities that are not inhabited, when I bring up the deep over you, and the great waters cover you,

20 Then I will make you go down with those who go down to the pit, to the people of old, and I will make you to dwell in the world below, among ruins from of old, with those who go down to the pit, so that you will not be inhabited, and I will set beauty in the land of the living.

21 I will bring you to a dreadful end, and you shall be no more. Though you are sought, you will never be found again, declares the Creator YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS preserves Chapter 26 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The oracle against Tzor — the great Phoenician maritime city-state — opens with Tzor's covenant offense: commercial gloating over Yerushalayim's fall — she saw the destruction of her trade competitor and said Aha. YHWH's covenant response to Tzor's commercial covenant opportunism is the comprehensive destruction of everything that made Tzor commercially dominant.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 26 in full. Verse 13 — I will stop the music of your songs, and the sound of your lyres shall be heard no more. The covenant silencing of Tzor's cultural life — the music that marked the city's prosperity — is part of the covenant comprehensive judgment. The covenant judgment reaches into every dimension of Tzor's existence: military, commercial, architectural, cultural.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 26. Verse 4 — I will scrape her soil from her and make her a bare rock. The covenant fulfillment noted by covenant historians: when Alexander the Great finally destroyed the island of Tzor in 332 BCE, he used the rubble of the mainland city to build a causeway to the island — literally scraping the soil of the mainland city into the sea.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 2 — because Tzor said concerning Yerushalayim, the gate of the peoples is broken, it has swung open to me. The covenant mercantile analysis behind Tzor's gloating: Yerushalayim controlled land trade routes between Mesopotamia and Egypt. With Yerushalayim eliminated, Tzor expected to capture that trade. YHWH's covenant judgment is economically precise: Tzor's covenant sin is commercial opportunism at the covenant people's expense, and the covenant judgment destroys the commercial power that motivated the gloating.

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTER 27 The Lamentation Over Tzor — A Ship of Perfect Beauty — Your Rowers Have Brought You Into the Great Waters — The Merchants Hiss at You

1 The word of YHWH came to me:

2 Now you, son of man, raise a lamentation over Tzor,

3 And say to Tzor, who dwells at the entrances to the sea, merchant of the peoples to many coastlands, thus says the Creator YHWH: O Tzor, you have said, I am perfect in beauty.

4 Your borders are in the heart of the seas. Your builders made perfect your beauty.

5 They made all your planks of fir trees from Senir. They took a cedar from Lebanon to make a mast for you.

6 Of oaks from Bashan they made your oars. They made your deck of pines from the coasts of Cyprus, inlaid with ivory.

7 Of fine embroidered linen from Egypt was your sail, serving as your banner. Blue and purple from the coasts of Elisha was your awning.

8 The inhabitants of Tzidon and Arvad were your rowers. Your skilled men, O Tzor, were in you. They were your pilots.

9 The elders of Gevul and her skilled men were in you, caulking your seams. All the ships of the sea with their mariners were in you to barter for your wares.

10 Persia and Lud and Put were in your army as your men of war. They hung the shield and helmet in you. They gave you splendor.

11 The men of Arvad and Helekh were on your walls all around, and the Gimmadites were in your towers. They hung their shields on your walls all around. They perfected your beauty.

12 Tarshish did business with you because of your great wealth of every kind. Silver, iron, tin, and lead they exchanged for your wares.

13 Yavan, Tuval, and Meshekh traded with you. They exchanged human beings and vessels of bronze for your merchandise.

14 From Beit-Togarmah they exchanged horses, war horses, and mules for your wares.

15 The men of Dedan traded with you. Many coastlands were your own special markets. They brought you in payment ivory tusks and ebony.

16 Aram did business with you because of your abundant goods. They exchanged for your wares emeralds, purple, embroidered work, fine linen, coral, and ruby.

17 Yehudah and the land of Yisra'EL traded with you. They exchanged for your merchandise wheat of Minnit, cakes, honey, oil, and balm.

18 Damascus did business with you for your abundant goods, because of your great wealth of every kind. Wine of Helbon and white wool they exchanged for your wares.

19 Vedan and Yavan from Uzal traded for your merchandise. Wrought iron, cassia, and calamus were bartered for your wares.

20 Dedan traded with you in saddlecloths for riding.

21 Arabia and all the princes of Kedar were your favored dealers in lambs, rams, and goats. In these they did business with you.

22 The traders of Sheba and Raamah traded with you. They exchanged for your wares the best of all kinds of spices and all precious stones and gold.

23 Haran, Kanneh, Eden, traders of Sheba, Assyria, and Kilmad traded with you.

24 In your market these traded with you in choice garments, in clothes of blue and embroidered work, and in carpets of colored material, bound with cords and made secure, in your market.

25 The ships of Tarshish traveled for you with your merchandise. So you were filled and heavily laden in the heart of the seas.

26 Your rowers have brought you out into the high seas. The east wind has wrecked you in the heart of the seas.

27 Your riches, your wares, your merchandise, your mariners and your pilots, your caulkers, your dealers in merchandise, and all your men of war who are in you, with all your crew that is in your midst, sink into the heart of the seas on the day of your fall.

28 At the sound of the cry of your pilots the countryside shakes,

29 And down from their ships come all who handle the oar, the mariners and all the pilots of the sea. They stand on the land

30 And shout aloud over you and cry out bitterly. They cast dust on their heads and wallow in ashes.

31 They make themselves bald for you and put on sackcloth, and they weep over you in bitterness of soul, with bitter mourning.

32 In their wailing they raise a lamentation for you and lament over you: Who is like Tzor, like one destroyed in the midst of the sea?

33 When your wares came from the seas, you satisfied many peoples. With your abundant wealth and merchandise you enriched the kings of the earth.

34 Now you are wrecked by the seas, in the depths of the waters. Your merchandise and all your crew in your midst have sunk with you.

35 All the inhabitants of the coastlands are appalled at you, and their kings are horribly afraid. Their faces are convulsed.

36 The merchants among the peoples hiss at you. You have come to a dreadful end and shall be no more forever.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS preserves Chapter 27 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The trade catalog of Chapter 27 — listing Tzor's trading partners across the ancient world — is one of the most comprehensive economic documents in the entire Hebrew canon. It is simultaneously a covenant lament and a covenant economic record: the very comprehensiveness of Tzor's trade network is the measure of her coming covenant loss.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 27 in full. Verse 10 — Persia and Lud and Put were in your army. The mercenaries of Tzor include Put — a northeast African people connected to Libya or Somalia in the ancient Afro-Asiatic corridor. The covenant catalog of nations touches the full extent of the ancient world known to the Orit tradition.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 27. Verse 17 — Yehudah and the land of Yisra'EL traded with you, exchanging wheat, cakes, honey, oil, and balm. The inclusion of Yehudah in the trade catalog — as a minor agricultural trading partner of the great maritime city — is the covenant humiliation embedded in the economic record: the covenant people were clients of the covenant city being judged.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 26-27 — your rowers have brought you into the high seas, and the east wind has wrecked you in the heart of the seas. All your riches sink with you on the day of your fall. The covenant ship metaphor: Tzor's commercial ambition drove the ship into the open sea where the covenant east wind — the covenant instrument — wrecks it. The very engine of Tzor's prosperity — the network of trade relations cataloged in verses 12-24 — becomes the weight that takes the whole structure to the bottom.

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTER 28 The Prince of Tzor — I Am a God — You Were the Signet of Perfection — You Were in Eden — Your Heart Was Proud — I Will Bring Strangers Upon You — Oracle Against Tzidon

1 The word of YHWH came to me:

2 Son of man, say to the prince of Tzor, Thus says the Creator YHWH: Because your heart is proud, and you have said, I am a god, I sit in the seat of the gods, in the heart of the seas, yet you are but a man, and no god, though you make your heart like the heart of a god —

3 You are indeed wiser than Daniyel. No secret is hidden from you.

4 By your wisdom and your understanding you have made wealth for yourself, and have gathered gold and silver into your treasuries.

5 By your great wisdom in your trade you have increased your wealth, and your heart has become proud in your wealth —

6 Therefore thus says the Creator YHWH: Because you make your heart like the heart of a god,

7 Therefore, behold, I will bring foreigners upon you, the most ruthless of the nations, and they shall draw their swords against the beauty of your wisdom and defile your splendor.

8 They shall thrust you down into the pit, and you shall die the death of the slain in the heart of the seas.

9 Will you still say, I am a god, in the presence of those who kill you, though you are but a man, and no god, in the hands of those who slay you?

10 You shall die the death of the uncircumcised by the hand of foreigners, for I have spoken, declares the Creator YHWH.

11 Moreover, the word of YHWH came to me:

12 Son of man, raise a lamentation over the king of Tzor, and say to him, Thus says the Creator YHWH: You were the signet of perfection, full of wisdom and perfect in beauty.

13 You were in Eden, the garden of YHWH. Every precious stone was your covering, sardius, topaz, and diamond, beryl, onyx, and jasper, sapphire, emerald, and carbuncle. And crafted in gold were your settings and your engravings. On the day that you were created they were prepared.

14 You were an anointed guardian cherub. I placed you. You were on the holy mountain of YHWH. You walked in the midst of the stones of fire.

15 You were blameless in your ways from the day you were created, till unrighteousness was found in you.

16 In the abundance of your trade you were filled with violence in your midst, and you sinned. So I cast you as a profane thing from the mountain of YHWH, and I destroyed you, O guardian cherub, from the midst of the stones of fire.

17 Your heart was proud because of your beauty. You corrupted your wisdom for the sake of your splendor. I cast you to the ground. I exposed you before kings, to feast their eyes on you.

18 By the multitude of your iniquities, in the unrighteousness of your trade you profaned your sanctuaries. So I brought fire out from your midst. It consumed you, and I turned you to ashes on the earth in the sight of all who saw you.

19 All who know you among the peoples are appalled at you. You have come to a dreadful end and shall be no more forever.

20 The word of YHWH came to me:

21 Son of man, set your face toward Tzidon, and prophesy against her

22 And say, Thus says the Creator YHWH: Behold, I am against you, O Tzidon, and I will manifest my glory in your midst. And they shall know that I am YHWH when I execute judgments in her and manifest my holiness in her.

23 For I will send pestilence into her, and blood into her streets, and the slain shall fall in her midst, by the sword that is against her on every side. Then they will know that I am YHWH.

24 And for the house of Yisra'EL there shall be no more a brier to prick or a thorn to hurt them among all their neighbors who have treated them with contempt. Then they will know that I am the Creator YHWH.

25 Thus says the Creator YHWH: When I gather the house of Yisra'EL from the peoples among whom they are scattered, and manifest my holiness in them in the sight of the nations, then they shall dwell in their own land that I gave to my servant Yaakov.

26 And they shall dwell securely in it, and they shall build houses and plant vineyards. They shall dwell securely, when I execute judgments upon all their neighbors who have treated them with contempt. Then they will know that I am YHWH their Creator.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS preserves Chapter 28 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The oracle against the king of Tzor — verses 12-19 — is the most cosmologically elevated passage in the book: the king is addressed using the language of the primordial guardian cherub in Eden, who was perfect and blameless until pride corrupted his wisdom. The cosmic-historical register of the oracle goes beyond historical-political commentary into covenant ontological analysis of the origin of pride.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 28 in full. Verse 13 — you were in Eden, the garden of YHWH. Every precious stone was your covering. The covenant Eden language applied to the king of Tzor is the Orit tradition's recognition of Eden as the covenant paradigm of created perfection — before corruption. The king of Tzor is portrayed as having occupied a cosmic position analogous to the original covenant dignity of creation.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 28. Verse 14 — you were an anointed guardian cherub. I placed you. You were on the holy mountain of YHWH. The cosmic scope of the oracle: the king of Tzor is addressed in terms that exceed any human king. The covenant interpretation is that the oracle speaks simultaneously to the human king and to the cosmic reality of pride's origin that the king embodies.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 15-17 — you were blameless from the day you were created, till unrighteousness was found in you. In the abundance of your trade you were filled with violence. Your heart was proud because of your beauty. You corrupted your wisdom for the sake of your splendor. The covenant anatomy of pride's corruption: the sequence is gift — beauty, wisdom, trade abundance — pride in the gift — corruption of the gift — violence — expulsion. The covenant pattern of creation, gift, pride, corruption, judgment is here given its most cosmic articulation.

THE ORACLES AGAINST EGYPT — CHAPTERS 29 THROUGH 32

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTER 29 The Oracle Against Egypt — Pharaoh the Great Dragon — The Broken Reed — Egypt Shall Be a Desolate Wasteland — Forty Years — I Give Egypt to Nevukhadnetzar

1 In the tenth year, in the tenth month, on the twelfth day of the month, the word of YHWH came to me:

2 Son of man, set your face against Pharaoh king of Egypt, and prophesy against him and against all Egypt.

3 Speak, and say, Thus says the Creator YHWH: Behold, I am against you, Pharaoh king of Egypt, the great dragon that lies in the midst of his streams, who says, My Nile is my own. I made it for myself.

4 I will put hooks in your jaws, and make the fish of your streams stick to your scales. And I will draw you up out of the midst of your streams, with all the fish of your streams that stick to your scales.

5 And I will cast you out into the wilderness, you and all the fish of your streams. You shall fall on the open field and not be brought together or gathered. To the beasts of the earth and to the birds of the heavens I give you as food.

6 Then all the inhabitants of Egypt shall know that I am YHWH, because they have been a staff of reed to the house of Yisra'EL.

7 When they grasped you with the hand, you broke and tore all their shoulders. And when they leaned on you, you broke and made all their loins to shake.

8 Therefore thus says the Creator YHWH: Behold, I will bring a sword upon you, and will cut off from you man and beast,

9 And the land of Egypt shall be a desolation and a waste. Then they will know that I am YHWH. Because you said, The Nile is mine, and I made it,

10 Therefore, behold, I am against you and against your streams, and I will make the land of Egypt an utter waste and desolation, from Migdol to Syene, as far as the border of Kush.

11 No foot of man shall pass through it, and no foot of beast shall pass through it. It shall be uninhabited forty years.

12 And I will make the land of Egypt a desolation in the midst of desolate countries, and her cities shall be a desolation forty years among cities that are laid waste. I will scatter the Egyptians among the nations and disperse them through the countries.

13 For thus says the Creator YHWH: At the end of forty years I will gather the Egyptians from the peoples among whom they were scattered,

14 And I will restore the fortunes of Egypt and bring them back to the land of Patros, the land of their origin, and there they shall be a lowly kingdom.

15 It shall be the most lowly of the kingdoms, and never again exalt itself above the nations. And I will make them so small that they will never again rule over the nations.

16 And it shall never again be the reliance of the house of Yisra'EL, recalling their iniquity, when they turn to them for aid. Then they will know that I am the Creator YHWH.

17 In the twenty-seventh year, in the first month, on the first day of the month, the word of YHWH came to me:

18 Son of man, Nevukhadnetzar king of Babylon made his army labor hard against Tzor. Every head was made bald and every shoulder was rubbed bare, yet neither he nor his army got anything from Tzor to pay for the labor that he had expended against it.

19 Therefore thus says the Creator YHWH: Behold, I will give the land of Egypt to Nevukhadnetzar king of Babylon, and he shall carry off its wealth and despoil it and plunder it, and it shall be the wages for his army.

20 I have given him the land of Egypt as his payment for which he labored, because they worked for me, declares the Creator YHWH.

21 On that day I will cause a horn to spring up for the house of Yisra'EL, and I will open your lips among them. Then they will know that I am YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS preserves Chapter 29 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The oracle against Egypt opens with Pharaoh as the great dragon — tannin — lying in the Nile, claiming ownership of the river: My Nile is my own, I made it for myself. This is the covenant claim of the self-created god-king against YHWH's covenant sovereignty over creation. The Nile — source of all Egyptian life and civilization — is YHWH's, not Pharaoh's.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 29 in full. Verse 14 — I will restore the fortunes of Egypt and bring them back to the land of Patros, the land of their origin — and there they shall be a lowly kingdom. The Orit tradition identifies Patros — Upper Egypt — as the original homeland of the Egyptian people. The covenant restoration of Egypt — like the covenant restoration promises given to other nations — is limited: they return but as a lowly kingdom, never again a dominant empire.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 29. Verse 6-7 — Egypt has been a staff of reed to the house of Yisra'EL. When grasped, it broke and tore their shoulders. The covenant evaluation of Egypt as a political alliance partner: the covenant community repeatedly sought Egyptian military backing against Babylon — and Egypt consistently failed to deliver. The covenant reed-staff breaks under the weight of covenant dependence.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 17-20 — the latest dated oracle in the entire book — the twenty-seventh year — 570 BCE — acknowledging that Nevukhadnetzar's siege of Tzor did not produce the expected plunder. YHWH's covenant explanation: therefore I give him Egypt as compensation. The covenant honesty of this oracle is remarkable: the covenant prophet acknowledges that the covenant word about Tzor's plunder did not materialize as expected, and gives a covenant theological explanation for the discrepancy.

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTER 30 Wail for the Day — The Day of YHWH Is Near — Egypt and Her Allies — I Will Break Pharaoh's Arms — The Arms of the King of Babylon Shall Be Strengthened

1 The word of YHWH came to me:

2 Son of man, prophesy, and say, Thus says the Creator YHWH: Wail, Alas for the day.

3 For the day is near, the day of YHWH is near. It will be a day of clouds, a time of doom for the nations.

4 A sword shall come upon Egypt, and anguish shall be in Kush, when the slain fall in Egypt, and her wealth is carried away, and her foundations are torn down.

5 Kush, and Put, and Lud, and all Arabia, and Libya, and the people of the land that is in league shall fall with them by the sword.

6 Thus says YHWH: Those who support Egypt shall fall, and her proud might shall come down. From Migdol to Syene they shall fall within her by the sword, declares the Creator YHWH.

7 And they shall be desolated in the midst of desolated countries, and their cities shall be in the midst of cities that are laid waste.

8 Then they will know that I am YHWH, when I have set fire to Egypt, and all her helpers are broken.

9 On that day messengers shall go out from me in ships to frighten the unsuspecting people of Kush, and anguish shall come upon them on the day of Egypt's doom. For, behold, it comes.

10 Thus says the Creator YHWH: I will put an end to the wealth of Egypt, by the hand of Nevukhadnetzar king of Babylon.

11 He and his people with him, the most ruthless of nations, shall be brought in to destroy the land, and they shall draw their swords against Egypt and fill the land with the slain.

12 And I will dry up the Nile and will sell the land into the hand of evildoers. I will bring desolation upon the land and everything in it, by the hand of foreigners. I am YHWH. I have spoken.

13 Thus says the Creator YHWH: I will destroy the idols and put an end to the images in Nof. There shall no longer be a prince from the land of Egypt. So I will put fear in the land of Egypt.

14 I will make Patros a desolation and will set fire to Tzo'an and will execute judgments on No.

15 And I will pour out my wrath upon Sin, the stronghold of Egypt, and cut off the multitude of No.

16 And I will set fire to Egypt. Sin shall be in great anguish. No shall be breached. Nof shall have adversaries by day.

17 The young men of Aven and Pi-beseth shall fall by the sword, and the cities themselves shall go into captivity.

18 At Tekhafnekhes the day shall be dark, when I break there the yoke-bars of Egypt, and her proud might shall come to an end in her. She shall be covered by a cloud, and her daughters shall go into captivity.

19 Thus I will execute judgments on Egypt. Then they will know that I am YHWH.

20 In the eleventh year, in the first month, on the seventh day of the month, the word of YHWH came to me:

21 Son of man, I have broken the arm of Pharaoh king of Egypt, and behold, it has not been bound up, to heal it by binding it with a bandage, so that it may become strong to wield the sword.

22 Therefore thus says the Creator YHWH: Behold, I am against Pharaoh king of Egypt and will break his arms, both the strong arm and the one that was broken, and I will make the sword fall from his hand.

23 I will scatter the Egyptians among the nations and disperse them through the countries.

24 And I will strengthen the arms of the king of Babylon and put my sword in his hand. But I will break the arms of Pharaoh, and he will groan before the king of Babylon like a man mortally wounded.

25 I will strengthen the arms of the king of Babylon, but the arms of Pharaoh shall fall. And they shall know that I am YHWH, when I put my sword into the hand of the king of Babylon and he stretches it out against the land of Egypt.

26 And I will scatter the Egyptians among the nations and disperse them throughout the countries. Then they will know that I am YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS preserves Chapter 30 in close agreement with the Masoretic. Chapter 30 is the covenant expansion of the judgment against Egypt into a regional oracle: Egypt's allies — Kush, Put, Lud, Libya — fall with Egypt. The covenant judgment is not confined to Egypt itself but extends to every nation whose covenant identity is bound up with Egypt's military and political power.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 30 in full and gives particular weight to verse 4 — a sword shall come upon Egypt, and anguish shall be in Kush. The covenant connection between Egypt and Kush — the northeast African corridor — is explicit: Egypt's fall sends anguish southward through the Kushite kingdoms. The Orit tradition recognizes the geopolitical unity of the Nile Valley covenant zone.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 30. Verse 21-26 — the broken arm of Pharaoh. YHWH has already broken one arm of Pharaoh — the military expedition that briefly interrupted the Babylonian siege of Yerushalayim — Chapter 37:5-8. Now YHWH will break both arms, while simultaneously strengthening the arms of Nevukhadnetzar. The covenant military transfer is explicit: YHWH's sword moves from Pharaoh's hand to Nevukhadnetzar's.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 13 — I will destroy the idols and put an end to the images in Nof — Memphis, the ancient capital of Egypt and center of Ptah worship. The covenant targeting of the theological center of Egyptian religious power: YHWH's covenant judgment against Egypt is simultaneously a covenant judgment against the Egyptian theological system. The idols and images of Memphis — representing one of the world's oldest and most sophisticated covenant systems of knowledge — are explicitly targeted for destruction.

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTER 31 The Cedar of Lebanon — Pharaoh Is Like Assyria — No Tree in Eden Was Its Equal — It Was Brought Down to Sheol

1 In the eleventh year, in the third month, on the first day of the month, the word of YHWH came to me:

2 Son of man, say to Pharaoh king of Egypt and to his multitude: Whom are you like in your greatness?

3 Behold, Assyria was a cedar in Lebanon, with beautiful branches and forest shade, and of towering height, its top among the clouds.

4 The waters nourished it. The deep made it grow tall, making its rivers flow around the place of its planting, sending forth its streams to all the trees of the field.

5 So it towered high above all the trees of the field. Its boughs grew large and its branches long from abundant water in its shoots.

6 All the birds of the heavens made their nests in its boughs. Under its branches all the beasts of the field gave birth to their young, and under its shadow lived all great nations.

7 It was beautiful in its greatness, in the length of its branches, for its roots went down to abundant waters.

8 The cedars in the garden of YHWH could not rival it, nor the fir trees equal its boughs. Neither were the plane trees like its branches. No tree in the garden of YHWH was its equal in beauty.

9 I made it beautiful in the mass of its branches, and all the trees of Eden envied it, that were in the garden of YHWH.

10 Therefore thus says the Creator YHWH: Because it towered high and set its top among the clouds, and its heart was proud of its height,

11 I will give it into the hand of a mighty one of the nations. He shall surely deal with it as its wickedness deserves. I have cast it out.

12 Foreigners, the most ruthless of nations, have cut it down and left it. On the mountains and in all the valleys its branches have fallen, and its boughs have been broken in all the ravines of the land. All the peoples of the earth have gone away from its shadow and left it.

13 On its fallen trunk dwell all the birds of the heavens, and on its branches are all the beasts of the field.

14 All this is in order that no trees by the waters may grow to towering height or set their tops among the clouds, and that no trees that drink water may reach up to them in height. For they are all given over to death, to the world below, among the children of man, with those who go down to the pit.

15 Thus says the Creator YHWH: On the day the cedar went down to Sheol I caused mourning. I closed the deep over it, and restrained its rivers, and the great waters were stopped. I clothed Lebanon in gloom for it, and all the trees of the field fainted because of it.

16 I made the nations quake at the sound of its fall, when I cast it down to Sheol with those who go down to the pit. And all the trees of Eden, the choicest and best of Lebanon, all that drink water, were comforted in the world below.

17 They also went down to Sheol with it, to those who are slain by the sword. Yes, those who were its arm, who lived under its shadow among the nations.

18 Whom are you thus like in glory and in greatness among the trees of Eden? You shall be brought down with the trees of Eden to the world below. You shall lie among the uncircumcised, with those who are slain by the sword. This is Pharaoh and all his multitude, declares the Creator YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS preserves Chapter 31 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The cedar of Lebanon oracle uses Assyria — already fallen — as the covenant cautionary example for Egypt: even the greatest empire of the preceding century was brought down from its cosmic height. Egypt, which rivals or exceeds Assyria in magnificence, faces the same covenant fate. The covenant principle: no created power — however magnificent — can occupy the height that belongs to YHWH alone.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 31 in full. Verse 8-9 — the cedars in the garden of YHWH could not rival it, and all the trees of Eden envied it. The cosmic Eden register of the oracle: the covenant tree of empire is compared to the trees of the garden of YHWH — and found to surpass them. The pride of the cosmic comparison is precisely what triggers the covenant judgment: nothing created is permitted to surpass the covenant garden.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 31. Verse 14 — all this is in order that no trees by the waters may grow to towering height, for they are all given over to death, to the world below. The covenant pedagogical purpose of empire's fall: the destruction of the great cedar is a covenant lesson for all remaining powers — height draws covenant judgment, not covenant protection.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 10 — because its heart was proud of its height. The covenant diagnosis of empire's fatal covenant error is consistent across Chapters 28-31: pride in created excellence. The cedar was genuinely magnificent — YHWH made it so, verse 9 — but the covenant inversion of beauty into pride is the covenant pattern that triggers the covenant fall. YHWH gives magnificence; pride converts it into self-assertion against the covenant Giver.

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTER 32 Lamentation Over Pharaoh — A Dragon in the Seas — Egypt Laid Low Among the Nations — The Mighty Chiefs Who Have Gone Down to Sheol

1 In the twelfth year, in the twelfth month, on the first day of the month, the word of YHWH came to me:

2 Son of man, raise a lamentation over Pharaoh king of Egypt and say to him: You consider yourself a lion of the nations, but you are like a dragon in the seas. You burst forth in your rivers, trouble the waters with your feet, and foul their rivers.

3 Thus says the Creator YHWH: I will throw my net over you with a host of many peoples, and they will haul you up in my dragnet.

4 And I will cast you on the ground. On the open field I will fling you, and will cause all the birds of the heavens to settle on you, and I will gorge the beasts of the whole earth with you.

5 I will strew your flesh upon the mountains and fill the valleys with your carcass.

6 I will drench the land even to the mountains with your flowing blood, and the ravines will be full of you.

7 When I blot you out, I will cover the heavens and make their stars dark. I will cover the sun with a cloud, and the moon shall not give its light.

8 All the bright lights of heaven will I make dark over you, and put darkness on your land, declares the Creator YHWH.

9 I will trouble the hearts of many peoples, when I carry you captive among the nations, into the countries that you have not known.

10 I will make many peoples appalled at you, and the hair of their kings shall bristle with horror because of you, when I brandish my sword before them. They shall tremble every moment, every one for his own life, on the day of your fall.

11 For thus says the Creator YHWH: The sword of the king of Babylon shall come upon you.

12 I will cause your multitude to fall by the swords of mighty ones, all of them most ruthless of nations. They shall devastate the pride of Egypt, and all its multitude shall perish.

13 I will destroy all its beasts from beside many waters. No foot of man shall trouble them anymore, nor shall the hoofs of beasts trouble them.

14 Then I will let their waters settle, and make their rivers run like oil, declares the Creator YHWH.

15 When I make the land of Egypt desolate, and when the land is desolate of all that fills it, when I strike down all who dwell in it, then they will know that I am YHWH.

16 This is a lamentation that shall be chanted. The daughters of the nations shall chant it. Over Egypt, and over all her multitude, shall they chant it, declares the Creator YHWH.

17 In the twelfth year, in the twelfth month, on the fifteenth day of the month, the word of YHWH came to me:

18 Son of man, wail over the multitude of Egypt, and send them down, her and the daughters of majestic nations, to the world below, to those who have gone down to the pit.

19 Whom do you surpass in beauty? Go down and be laid to rest with the uncircumcised.

20 They shall fall amid those who are slain by the sword. Egypt is delivered to the sword. Drag her away and all her multitudes.

21 The mighty chiefs shall speak of them, with their helpers, out of the midst of Sheol: They have come down. They lie still, the uncircumcised, slain by the sword.

22 Assyria is there, and all her company, its graves all around it, all of them slain, fallen by the sword,

23 Whose graves are set in the uttermost parts of the pit, and her company is all around her grave, all of them slain, fallen by the sword, who spread terror in the land of the living.

24 Eilam is there, and all her multitude around her grave, all of them slain, fallen by the sword, who went down uncircumcised into the world below, who spread their terror in the land of the living. And they bear their shame with those who go down to the pit.

25 They have made her a bed among the slain with all her multitude, her graves all around it, all of them uncircumcised, slain by the sword. For terror of them was spread in the land of the living, and they bear their shame with those who go down to the pit. They are placed among the slain.

26 Meshekh-Tuval is there, and all her multitude, her graves all around it, all of them uncircumcised, slain by the sword, for they spread their terror in the land of the living.

27 And they do not lie with the mighty, the fallen from among the uncircumcised, who went down to Sheol with their weapons of war, whose swords were laid under their heads, and whose iniquities are upon their bones. For the terror of the mighty men was in the land of the living.

28 But as for you, you shall be broken and lie among the uncircumcised, with those who are slain by the sword.

29 Edom is there, her kings and all her princes, who for all their might are laid with those who are slain by the sword. They lie with the uncircumcised, with those who go down to the pit.

30 The princes of the north are there, all of them, and all the Tzidonians, who have gone down in shame with the slain, for all the terror that they caused by their might. They lie uncircumcised with those who are slain by the sword, and bear their shame with those who go down to the pit.

31 When Pharaoh sees them, he will be comforted for all his multitude, Pharaoh and all his army, slain by the sword, declares the Creator YHWH.

32 For I spread terror of him in the land of the living, and he shall be laid to rest among the uncircumcised, with those who are slain by the sword, Pharaoh and all his multitude, declares the Creator YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS preserves Chapter 32 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The descent of Egypt to Sheol — joining Assyria, Eilam, Meshekh-Tuval, Edom, Tzidon — is the covenant underworld catalog of fallen empires. Egypt will find in Sheol the company of every great power that has already been brought down. The covenant leveling of all imperial greatness in the world below is the final covenant statement of the oracle against Egypt.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 32 in full. Verse 7-8 — when I blot you out, I will cover the heavens and make their stars dark. The cosmic scale of the covenant judgment against Egypt: the death of Egypt darkens the heavens. The covenant imagery links Egypt's fall to the Day of YHWH language of darkened luminaries — the cosmic convulsion that accompanies covenant judgment at the maximum level.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 32. Verse 21 — the mighty chiefs shall speak of them out of the midst of Sheol: they have come down. The covenant descent narrative — Egypt joins the roll of fallen empires in the world below. Assyria — verse 22 — was the greatest power of the preceding century. Egypt now joins Assyria, Eilam, Meshekh, Edom, and Tzidon in the covenant underworld of fallen imperial greatness.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 31 — when Pharaoh sees them, he will be comforted for all his multitude. The covenant irony of the Sheol catalog: Pharaoh is comforted in death by the discovery that he is not alone — every great empire that terrorized the land of the living is already there. The covenant community of the dead contains all the former oppressors of the covenant people. The covenant leveling of Sheol is the covenant justice that YHWH's living covenant community cannot yet see from the perspective of history.

END OF PART FOUR — CHAPTERS 25 THROUGH 32

𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄
